title: The Latent Class Model
text: Latent Class Analysis assumes people belong different groups (.e., classes) due nonobservable characteristics. latent class model, aim estimate two kinds probabilities: probability person belongs particular class. conditional probabilities item responses person belongs given class.

title: The Latent Class Model
text: Suppose sample people respond JJ items 𝐲\mathbf{y} vector contains scores item jj. Let KK denote number latent classes let xkx_k class kk. , likelihood response pattern 𝐲\mathbf{y}, observed nn times sample every person responds independently , can written ℓ=P(𝐲)n=(∑k=1KP(xk)P(𝐲∣xk))n. \ell \;=\; P(\mathbf{y})^{n} \;=\; \Bigg(\sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, P(\mathbf{y}\mid x_k)\Bigg)^{n}. Assuming local independence (responses within person independent conditional class), P(𝐲∣xk)=∏j=1JP(yj∣xk), P(\mathbf{y}\mid x_k) \;=\; \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k),  yjy_j denotes score item jj. Hence, ℓ=(∑k=1KP(xk)∏j=1JP(yj∣xk))n, \ell \;=\; \Bigg(\sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k)\Bigg)^{\!n},  log-likelihood becomes ℓℓ=nlog⁡(∑k=1KP(xk)∏j=1JP(yj∣xk)). \ell\ell \;=\; n \log \Bigg(\sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k)\Bigg).  term inside parenthesis probability single pattern, P(𝐲)P(\mathbf{y}). Assuming independence people different response patterns, log-likelihood whole sample sum log-likelihoods response pattern. simplify computation logarithm likelihood related derivatives, let lym∣xg=log⁡P(ym∣xg)l_{y_m \mid x_g} = \log P(y_m \mid x_g), ℓℓ=nlog⁡(∑k=1KP(xk)exp⁡(∑j=1Jlym∣xg)). \ell\ell \;=\; n \log \Bigg(\sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \exp\bigg(\sum_{j=1}^{J} l_{y_m \mid x_g}\bigg)\Bigg).

title: The Latent Class Model
text: fixed pattern 𝐲\mathbf{y}, define P(𝐲)=∑k=1KP(xk)∏j=1JP(yj∣xk)=∑k=1KP(xk)exp⁡(∑j=1Jlym∣xg). \begin{aligned} P(\mathbf{y}) \;&=\; \sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k) \\ &=\; \sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \exp\bigg(\sum_{j=1}^{J} l_{y_m \mid x_g}\bigg). \end{aligned}  ∂ℓℓ∂P(xg)=n1P(𝐲)∏j=1JP(yj∣xg). \frac{\partial \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_g)} \;=\; n \,\frac{1}{P(\mathbf{y})}\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g).  specific item mm class gg, ∂ℓℓ∂lym∣xg=n1P(𝐲)P(xg)∏j=1JP(yj∣xg). \frac{\partial \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} \;=\; n \,\frac{1}{P(\mathbf{y})}\, P(x_g)\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g). Notice last expression just posterior, P(xg∣ym)P(x_g \mid y_m), weighted nn.

text: d[∂ℓℓ∂P(xg)]=nP(𝐲)d[∏j=1JP(yj∣xg)]−d[P(𝐲)]∏j=1JP(yj∣xg)P(𝐲)2, d \Bigg[ \frac{\partial \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_g)} \Bigg] \;=\; n \,\frac{P(\mathbf{y}) \; d\big[\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g)\big] - d\big[P(\mathbf{y})\big] \; \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g)}{P(\mathbf{y})^2},  d[∏j=1JP(yj∣xg)]=∏j=1JP(yj∣xg)P(yj∣xg)d[P(yj∣xg)], d\big[\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g)\big] = \frac{\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g)}{P(y_j \mid x_g) \, d\big[P(y_j \mid x_g)\big]},  d[P(𝐲)]=∑k=1Kd[P(xk)]∏j=1JP(yj∣xk)+∑k=1KP(xk)d[∏j=1JP(yj∣xk)]. d\big[P(\mathbf{y})\big] = \sum_{k=1}^{K} d\big[P(x_k)] \, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k) + \sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k) \, d\big[\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k)].

title: The Latent Class Model
text: classes g,hg,h, ∂2ℓℓ∂P(xg)∂P(xh)=−n1P(𝐲)2(∏j=1JP(yj∣xg))(∏j=1JP(yj∣xh))=−nP(xg∣y)P(xh∣y)P(xg)P(xh). \begin{aligned} \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_g)\,\partial P(x_h)} \;&=\; -\,n \,\frac{1}{P(\mathbf{y})^{2}} \, \Bigg(\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g)\Bigg)\! \Bigg(\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_h)\Bigg) \\ &=\; -n \frac{P(x_g \mid y) P(x_h \mid y)}{P(x_g) P(x_h)}. \end{aligned} items m,nm,n classes g,hg,h, ∂2ℓℓ∂lym∣xg∂lyn∣xh={nP(xg∣y)(1−P(xg∣y)),g=h,−nP(xg∣y)P(xh∣y),otherwise. \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}\,\partial l_{y_n \mid x_h}} = \begin{cases} \,n \, P(x_g \mid y) \!\ \big(1-P(x_g \mid y)\big), & \text{} \!\ g=h,\\[1.25em] -\,n \, P(x_g \mid y) \!\ P(x_h \mid y), & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} mixed second derivative, ∂2ℓℓ∂P(xh)∂lym∣xg={1P(xg)nP(xg∣y)(1−P(xg∣y)),g=h,−1P(xh)nP(xg∣y)P(xh∣y),otherwise. \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_h)\,\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{P(x_g)} \, n \, P(x_g \mid y) \big(1-P(x_g \mid y)\big), & \text{} g=h,\\[1.0em] -\, \frac{1}{P(x_h)} \, n \, P(x_g \mid y) P(x_h \mid y), & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} Collecting terms gives Hessian block form: Hess(ℓℓ)=[∂2ℓℓ∂P(xg)∂P(xh)∂2ℓℓ∂P(xh)∂lym∣xg∂2ℓℓ∂lym∣xg∂P(xh)∂2ℓℓ∂lym∣xg∂lym∣xg]. \mathrm{Hess}(\ell\ell) \;=\; \begin{bmatrix} \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_g)\,\partial P(x_h)} & \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_h)\,\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} \\[0.8em] \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}\,\partial P(x_h)} & \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}\,\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} \end{bmatrix}\!.

text: conditional probabilities need parameterized likelihood. consider multinomial likelihood categorical items Gaussian likelihood continuous items. categorical items, let πmk∣g\pi_{m_k \mid g} probability scoring category kk item mm subject belongs class gg. P(ym∣xg)=πmk∣g, P(y_m \mid x_g) \;=\; \pi_{m_k \mid g},  kk ym=ky_m = k. parameterization, ∂lym∣xg∂πnk∣h={1πnk∣h,ym=k,m=n,g=h,0,otherwise. \frac{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \pi_{n_k \mid h}} = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{\pi_{n_k \mid h}}, & \text{} y_m = k,\ m=n,\ g=h,\\ 0, & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}  ∂2lyj∣xi∂πmk∣g∂πnl∣h={−1πmj∣i2,yj=k=l,y=m=n,=g=h,0,otherwise. \frac{\partial^2 l_{y_j \mid x_i}}{\partial \pi_{m_k \mid g} \partial \pi_{n_l \mid h}} = \begin{cases} -\frac{1}{\pi_{m_j \mid }^2}, & \text{} y_j = k = l,\ y=m=n,\ =g=h,\\ 0, & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} Consequently, Hessian conditional parameter following block form: Hess(lym∣xg)=𝐞∂2lyj∣xi∂πmk∣g∂πnl∣h𝐞⊤, \mathrm{Hess}(l_{y_m \mid x_g}) \;=\; \mathbf{e} \; \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 l_{y_j \mid x_i}}{\partial \pi_{m_k \mid g} \partial \pi_{n_l \mid h}} \; \mathbf{e}^\top,  𝐞\mathbf{e} vector zeroes 1 position corresponding parameter πyj∣\pi_{y_j \mid }. Notice conditional parameter lym∣xgl_{y_m \mid x_g} Hessian matrix. continuous items, let φ\varphi denote normal density. Let μm∣g\mu_{m\mid g} σm∣g\sigma_{m\mid g} mean standard deviation item mm class gg. P(ym∣xg)=φ(ym;μm∣g,σm∣g). P(y_m \mid x_g) \;=\; \varphi\!\big(y_m;\, \mu_{m\mid g},\, \sigma_{m\mid g}\big). First derivatives: ∂lym∣xg∂μn∣h={ym−μm∣gσm∣g2,m=n,g=h,0,otherwise, \frac{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \mu_{n\mid h}} = \begin{cases} \dfrac{y_m - \mu_{m\mid g}}{\sigma_{m\mid g}^{2}}, & \text{} m=n,\ g=h,\\[0.6em] 0, & \text{otherwise,} \end{cases} ∂lym∣xg∂σn∣h={(ym−μm∣g)2−σm∣g2σm∣g3,m=n,g=h,0,otherwise. \frac{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \sigma_{n\mid h}} = \begin{cases} \dfrac{(y_m-\mu_{m\mid g})^{2}-\sigma_{m\mid g}^{2}}{\sigma_{m\mid g}^{3}}, & \text{} m=n,\ g=h,\\[0.6em] 0, & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} Second-order derivatives: ∂2lym∣xg∂μm∣g∂μn∣h={−1σm∣g2,m=n,g=h,0,otherwise, \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \mu_{m\mid g}\, \partial \mu_{n\mid h}} = \begin{cases} -\dfrac{1}{\sigma_{m\mid g}^{2}}, & \text{} m=n,\ g=h,\\[0.6em] 0, & \text{otherwise,} \end{cases} ∂2lym∣xg∂σm∣g∂σn∣h={1σm∣g2−3(ym−μm∣g)2σm∣g4,m=n,g=h,0,otherwise, \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \sigma_{m\mid g}\, \partial \sigma_{n\mid h}} = \begin{cases} \dfrac{1}{\sigma_{m\mid g}^{2}} - \dfrac{3(y_m-\mu_{m\mid g})^{2}}{\sigma_{m\mid g}^{4}}, & \text{} m=n,\ g=h,\\[1.0em] 0, & \text{otherwise,} \end{cases} ∂2lym∣xg∂μm∣g∂σn∣h={−2(ym−μm∣g)σm∣g3,m=n,g=h,0,otherwise. \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \mu_{m\mid g}\, \partial \sigma_{n\mid h}} = \begin{cases} -\dfrac{2(y_m-\mu_{m\mid g})}{\sigma_{m\mid g}^{3}}, & \text{} m=n,\ g=h,\\[0.6em] 0, & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} Consequently, Hessian conditional parameter following block form: Hess(lym∣xg)=[∂2lym∣xg∂μm∣g∂μn∣h∂2lym∣xg∂μm∣g∂σn∣h∂2lym∣xg∂σn∣h∂μm∣g∂2lym∣xg∂σm∣g∂σn∣h]. \mathrm{Hess}(l_{y_m \mid x_g}) \;=\; \begin{bmatrix} \displaystyle \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \mu_{m \mid g}\,\partial \mu_{n \mid h}} & \displaystyle \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \mu_{m \mid g}\,\partial \sigma_{n \mid h}} \\[0.6em] \displaystyle \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \sigma_{n \mid h}\,\partial \mu_{m \mid g}} & \displaystyle \frac{\partial^{2} l_{y_m \mid x_g}}{\partial \sigma_{m \mid g}\,\partial \sigma_{n \mid h}} \end{bmatrix}. Notice conditional parameter lym∣xgl_{y_m \mid x_g} Hessian matrix.

text: fit object class "llca" returned lca. Character string indicating object extract. Available values aliases include: "profile" Posterior class sizes class-conditional     indicator profiles. distal outcomes present,     class-conditional profiles included outcomes element. "classconditional", "item", "items" Class-conditional indicator parameters. Gaussian indicators contain     means standard deviations; multinomial indicators contain     probabilities . "outcome", "outcomes" Class-conditional distal     outcome parameters. "class", "classes" Average model-implied prior class     probabilities, weighted response-pattern weights. "fullclasses" Pattern-specific prior class probabilities. "beta", "coef", "coefs" Class-membership     regression coefficients. "posterior" Case-specific posterior class probabilities. "state", "states" Modal posterior class assignments. "respconditional" fully multinomial measurement models,     probabilities latent-class membership conditional response     category. "probcat" fully multinomial measurement models,     marginal response-category probabilities. "data" Processed data stored fitted object. "measurement" Processed indicator data. "pattern", "patterns" Unique model-variable patterns     observed weights. "table", "summary" Response-pattern summary table. "loglik_case" Case-specific log-likelihood contributions. "loglik_pattern" Pattern-specific weighted     log-likelihood contributions. "loss", "loglik", "fit_matrix" Optimizer loss,     log-likelihood, complete matrix fit components. "classification" Modal proportional     classification-error matrices. Rows represent latent classes     columns represent assigned classes. "convergence" Optimizer convergence information. "gradient" Gradient diagnostics. "timing", "elapsed" Elapsed optimization time. Matching case-insensitive. digits Non-negative integer retained compatibility inspection methods. Numeric results returned without rounding can safely used subsequent computations. model object class "llcalist" containing fitted "llca" objects.

Class membership probabilities can unconditional modeled softmax transformation observed covariates. Distal outcomes can also included modeled conditionally latent classes. function supports one-step estimation, Bakk–Kuha two-step estimation, ML three-step estimation modal proportional classification, class enumeration passing several values nclasses.

title: Latent Class Analysis — lca
text: data data.frame containing observed variables. Variables listed gaussian /multinomial used indicators. Variables listed covariates used predict class membership. Variables listed outcomes treated distal outcomes. nclasses positive integer giving number latent classes. vector positive integers supplied, one model fitted value result returned object class "llcalist". usually termed "class enumeration". gaussian Optional character vector names variables data modeled Gaussian indicators. variables modeled class-specific means variances. residual covariance terms involving Gaussian indicators specified model, corresponding variables modeled jointly class-specific multivariate Gaussian distribution. multinomial Optional character vector names variables data modeled multinomial indicators. variables converted factors internally. first factor level used reference category softmax transformation. Variables 30 observed categories allowed estimated probabilities unreliable. covariates Optional character vector names variables data used predict latent class membership. Internally, intercept always included. Character covariates converted factors factors dummy-coded using model.matrix(). Observations missing values covariates removed estimation. outcomes Optional distal outcomes. can either character vector variable names data, named list specifying likelihood outcome. character vector supplied, numeric variables treated Gaussian outcomes non-numeric variables multinomial outcomes. list supplied, supported names gaussian multinomial. example, outcomes = c("y1", "y2", "z") automatically assigns numeric outcomes Gaussian likelihood non-numeric outcomes multinomial likelihood; outcomes = list(gaussian = c("y1", "y2")) adds Gaussian distal outcomes; outcomes = list(multinomial = "z") adds multinomial distal outcome. possible predict different types outcomes time using outcomes = list(gaussian = c("y1", "y2"), multinomial = "z") model Optional model specification. can named list used fix parameters, impose equality constraints, provide custom parameter labels. Names match internal parameter blocks, example beta, Gaussian multinomial item names, Sigma|Class<> multivariate Gaussian blocks. Character strings containing residual-dependency syntax "y1 ~~ y2" "u1 ~~ u2 ~~ u3" also used identify residual covariances residual associations. object class "llca" supplied, measurement parameters reused class-membership regression coefficients re-estimated. weights Optional numeric vector observation weights, one value per row data. adjustment Character string selecting estimation strategy covariates supplied. Use "none" one-step estimation, "bk" Bakk–Kuha two-step method, "ml" ML three-step correction based classification error. Defaults "bk". classification Character string used adjustment = "ml". Use "modal" modal assignment "prop" proportional assignment estimating classification-error matrix. penalties Logical value named list controlling regularization. FALSE, penalties used. TRUE, default penalties used. named list supplied, missing penalty blocks filled defaults. Valid penalty blocks beta, class, prob, var, Sigma. default list(beta = list(alpha = 0), class = list(alpha = 1), prob = list(alpha = 1), var = list(alpha = 1), Sigma = list(alpha = 1)). beta block may also contain lambda power ridge-type regularization. start Optional named list starting values. Names correspond parameter blocks model. Supplied values replace corresponding default initial values, allowing partial specification starting values. control Optional list optimizer estimation controls. Common entries include rstarts number random starts, cores parallel computation, maxit maximum number optimizer iterations, opt optimizer type, convergence tolerances eps, df_eps, step_eps. Missing entries replaced internal defaults. .fit Logical. TRUE, model estimated. FALSE, function returns "llca" object containing processed data, model structure, optimization setup, without running optimizer. verbose Logical. TRUE, progress information printed, especially fitting several values nclasses.

title: Latent Class Analysis — lca
text: Estimate latent class models continuous categorical indicators, optional covariates distal outcomes. measurement model defined variables supplied gaussian multinomial. Variables listed ignored measurement model unless used covariates distal outcomes. Gaussian indicators, model estimates class-specific means variances. Variances parameterized log-variances transformed positive scale optimization. multinomial indicators, model estimates class-specific category probabilities softmax parameterization. item stored one matrix: first rows contain probabilities following rows contain log parameters. first log parameter fixed zero identification. covariates supplied adjustment = "none", class probabilities modeled one step multinomial-log coefficients stored beta parameter block. first class reference class, coefficients fixed zero. adjustment = "bk", measurement model first estimated without covariates. structural model estimated measurement parameters fixed. adjustment = "ml", measurement model first estimated, cases assigned classes using either modal proportional classification, structural model estimated using classification-error correction. function compresses data unique response/covariate patterns uses frequencies pattern weights. observation weights supplied weights, incorporated pattern weights. Residual dependencies can requested covariance-style syntax model. Gaussian indicators, terms "y1 ~~ y2" define class-specific residual covariance parameters. multinomial indicators, dependency syntax defines joint categorical response blocks. example, "u1 ~~ u2" replaces locally independent likelihood contribution u1 u2 joint multinomial contribution u1.u2. Similarly, connected dependency "u1 ~~ u2 ~~ u3" defines one joint variable u1.u2.u3. Pairwise interaction parameters used build joint log-probabilities, marginal item probabilities recovered joint probabilities summing relevant joint levels.

title: Convert a fitted lcfa object (latent) to a lavaan object — lcfa_to_lavaan
text: function reconstructs lavaan S4 object fitted lcfa object, allowing use lavaan post-processing functions fitMeasures(), lavResiduals(), modindices(). conversion works : Building lavaan scaffold (un-fitted) model syntax, data,         estimator, options. title: Convert a fitted lcfa object (latent) to a lavaan object — lcfa_to_lavaan
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(latent) library(lavaan)  HS.model <- ' visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3               textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6               speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9 '  fit_latent <- lcfa(model = HS.model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939,                    estimator = "ml", std.lv = TRUE, meanstructure = TRUE) fit_lavaan <- lcfa_to_lavaan(fit_latent) fitMeasures(fit_lavaan) lavResiduals(fit_lavaan) modindices(fit_lavaan, sort = TRUE) } # }

title: Maximum likelihood estimation of positive-definite polychoric correlation matrices — lpoly
text: data data frame matrix containing raw ordinal data. method Character string indicating estimation method. Possible values "one-step" "two-step". Default "two-step". model Optional model object used internally initialization custom model setup. Default NULL. positive Logical. TRUE, estimated polychoric correlation matrix forced positive semidefinite. Default FALSE. penalties Logical. TRUE, penalties added objective function encourage positive-definite solution. Default FALSE. .fit Logical. TRUE, model fitted. FALSE, model setup returned. Default TRUE. message Logical. TRUE, progress messages printed estimation. title: Maximum likelihood estimation of positive-definite polychoric correlation matrices — lpoly
text: list containing fitted model related information. Typical elements include: version: Version number latent used model   estimated. call: Matched call used estimate model. ModelInfo: Information model specification data. Optim: Output optimization routine. parameters: Structure containing model parameters. transparameters: Structure containing transformed model   parameters. loglik: Log-likelihood fitted model. penalized_loglik: Penalized log-likelihood fitted model.

title: Maximum likelihood estimation of positive-definite polychoric correlation matrices — lpoly
text: lpoly estimates positive-definite positive-semidefinite polychoric correlation matrices ordinal data. function designed situations unrestricted polychoric matrix guaranteed admissible, example sampling variability sparse response patterns. Two estimation strategies available: "two-step": thresholds estimated first correlation   matrix estimated second step. "one-step": thresholds correlations estimated jointly. positive = TRUE, estimated matrix constrained positive semidefinite. penalties = TRUE, penalty terms added objective function encourage positive-definiteness. .fit = FALSE, function returns model setup without running optimizer.
